chunks.heading: Protocol Tracker
chunks.text: A **local-first** clinical protocol tracker. Add and track protocol records on your device; your data is stored **only** in the browser (IndexedDB) and **never** sent to a server. Back up and move data between devices with an **encrypted** file you control. Installable as a PWA — on iOS/Android, *Add to Home Screen* launches it full-screen like a native app, and it works offline.

chunks.heading: Why local-first
chunks.text: - **Privacy by design:** no server holds patient data, so the compliance surface shrinks dramatically. - **Portable:** encrypted backup/restore (`.ptbackup`) moves data device-to-device with a passphrase. - **Offline:** a service worker caches the app shell; it opens with no network.

chunks.heading: Encryption
chunks.text: Backups are encrypted entirely in the browser: `passphrase → PBKDF2-SHA256 (200k iterations) → AES-GCM-256`. The backup file contains only ciphertext, a random salt, and an IV — no readable data, and no key. Lose the passphrase and the backup is unrecoverable by design.

chunks.heading: Structure
chunks.text: | File | Role | |------|------| | `index.html` | UI shell | | `app.js` | records (IndexedDB) + encrypted backup/restore (WebCrypto) | | `styles.css` | styling | | `manifest.webmanifest` | PWA manifest (installable, standalone) | | `sw.js` | service worker (offline app-shell cache) | | `build_icons.js` | regenerates the PNG icons (`node build_icons.js`) |

chunks.heading: Protocol Tracker — v1 Data Contract (FROZEN)
chunks.text: > **Status:** frozen baseline as of app **v15**, `SCHEMA_VERSION = 1`. > This documents the on-device data shape so future changes stay **additive** and > `runMigrations()` (app.js) has a known starting point. **Do not change a v1 field's > meaning or type** — add new fields / a new `type`, bump `SCHEMA_VERSION`, and write an > ordered migration instead. Every claim here was read from `app.js` (not recalled).

chunks.heading: Storage engine
chunks.text: - **IndexedDB database:** `protocol-tracker`, **version `2`** (`app.js:6,11`). - **Object stores** (created in `onupgradeneeded`, `app.js:14-15`): - **`records`** — keyPath **`id`**. All user data (PHI) lives here, encrypted at rest. - **`meta`** — keyPath **`k`**. Small unencrypted control rows (auth params, schema version).

chunks.heading: Crypto (zero-knowledge, on-device only)
chunks.text: - **KDF:** PBKDF2-SHA-256, **`KDF_ITERS = 200000`** iterations (`app.js:40,45`). - **Cipher:** AES-GCM-256, random **12-byte IV** per encryption (`app.js:46,50`). - The passphrase is **never stored**. `meta.auth` holds only: `salt` (b64, 16 bytes), `iters` (the KDF iteration count used — read back on unlock so params can evolve without bricking existing data), and a `verifier` = the constant token **`protocol-tracker-verify-v1`** encrypted under the derived key (`app.js:64-74`). - Unlock re-derives the key and decrypts the verifier; match ⇒ correct passphrase (`app.js:78-87`). `unlock()` honors the **stored `iters`** (`meta.iters || KDF_ITERS`), not the current constant. - Session key lives **in memory only** (`sessionKey`); lost on close or Lock. **No recovery.** The encrypted backup/restore (below) is the only safety net.

chunks.heading: Record row shape (on disk, in `records`)
chunks.text: Every row is stored encrypted and looks like (`app.js:95-98`): ``` { id: <string, plaintext>, enc: 1, iv: <b64>, ct: <b64> } ``` - **`id`** stays plaintext (it is a random uuid or a derived key like `log-YYYY-MM-DD`, **never PHI**) so rows can be `put`/`delete`d by key. - **All other fields** live inside the AES-GCM ciphertext `ct`. Decrypted, a record has a **`type`** discriminator and the per-type fields below.

chunks.heading: Record types (decrypted payloads)
chunks.text: | `type` | `id` | Fields (verified from writers) | |-----------|---------------------|--------------------------------| | `profile` | `'profile'` (singleton) | `name`, `weight`, `weightUnit` (`'kg'` default), `condition` (`app.js:246-249`) | | `supp` | `uid()` | `suppId`/catalog ref, `name`, `form`, `dose`, `unit`, `dayParts[]`, `timingKey` (default `'with-food'`) (`app.js:364-371`) | | `log` | `'log-YYYY-MM-DD'` | `date`, `t0` (Start-my-day epoch ms, `null` until started), `planned` (dose count at T0 = adherence denominator), `taken` map (`app.js:189,422-424`) | | `symptom` | `uid()` | `date`, `ts` (ISO), `label`, `severity` (1–10 Number), `note` (`app.js:437`) | | `water` | `uid()` | `date`, `ts` (ISO), `ml` (`app.js:459`) | | `meal` | `uid()` | `date`, `ts` (ISO), `kind`, `note` (`app.js:478`) | - **`log.taken`** is a map keyed **`"<suppId>::<daypart>"`** ⇒ `true` (`app.js:568,956`). A dose is "taken" iff its `suppId::daypart` key is present. - **`log.planned`** is set once, at Start-my-day, to the number of scheduled dose-items that day

chunks.heading: Record types (decrypted payloads)
chunks.text: keyed **`"<suppId>::<daypart>"`** ⇒ `true` (`app.js:568,956`). A dose is "taken" iff its `suppId::daypart` key is present. - **`log.planned`** is set once, at Start-my-day, to the number of scheduled dose-items that day (`app.js:424`); it is the fixed denominator for adherence history.

chunks.heading: `meta` store rows
chunks.text: | `k` | Payload | |------------|---------| | `'auth'` | `{ k:'auth', v:1, salt, iters, verifier:{iv,ct} }` (`app.js:74`) | | `'schema'` | `{ k:'schema', v: SCHEMA_VERSION }` — current on-disk schema version (`app.js:203,209-210`) |

chunks.heading: Portable encrypted backup (cross-device)
chunks.text: `encryptBackup()` / `decryptBackup()` (`app.js:124-137`) produce a **self-describing** blob with its **own** passphrase (independent of the unlock passphrase): ``` { app: 'protocol-tracker', v: 1, kdf: { name: 'PBKDF2', hash: 'SHA-256', iters: 200000 }, salt: <b64, 16 bytes>, iv: <b64>, ciphertext: <b64>, // AES-GCM of the full records array createdAt: <ISO string> } ``` - Restore validates `blob.app === 'protocol-tracker'` before touching anything, and derives with the blob's **own** `kdf.iters` (falls back to `KDF_ITERS`) — so a backup taken under one iteration count still restores after the constant changes.

chunks.heading: Migration rule (how to evolve this contract)
chunks.text: 1. Never repurpose a v1 field. Add new fields / a new `type` only. 2. Bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` in `app.js`. 3. Add an ordered `cur -> SCHEMA_VERSION` step inside `runMigrations()` — it runs after every unlock, **behind decryption**, and must be **non-destructive** (`app.js:200-211`). 4. Add a harness check for the migration in `test/harness.mjs`.

chunks.heading: Device-to-device transfer (task #11) — design options, needs a decision
chunks.text: > **Status:** proposal only, nothing built. Written 2026-07-04 while the buildable > tasks (#7, #10) shipped. This one needs Cedric to pick a direction before code, > because the app is **zero-knowledge with no backend for user data** — there is no > server to relay a vault through, which is the whole difficulty.

chunks.heading: The constraint that shapes everything
chunks.text: - All user data is AES-GCM encrypted on-device; the server (`push.condaydigital.com`) only ever sees generic push schedules — never records. There is **no store-and-forward backend** for the vault. - A QR code holds ~2–3 KB of data. A real vault (logs, symptoms, months of history) is far larger. **So a QR code cannot carry the vault itself** — only a short secret or a URL.

chunks.heading: What we already have (the honest baseline)
chunks.text: The **encrypted backup/restore** (Manage → Export/Restore) *is already a device-to-device transfer*: export the `.ptbackup` file, move it by AirDrop / email / cloud / cable, restore on the new device with the backup passphrase. It works today and is covered by tests (`round-trip is loss-less`). Any #11 work should be measured against "is this meaningfully better than the backup file we already ship?"

chunks.heading: A. QR-of-passphrase, blob moves out-of-band  *(smallest, safe)*
chunks.text: Old device shows a QR encoding a **one-time transfer PIN**; it exports the vault encrypted under that PIN to a file. New device scans the QR (gets the PIN), user moves the file over, import auto-uses the scanned PIN. **Pro:** tiny delta on existing backup, no new backend, no new trust model. **Con:** user still moves a file manually — QR only saves typing a passphrase. Marginal.

chunks.heading: B. LAN / WebRTC direct transfer  *(best UX, most work)*
chunks.text: QR carries a WebRTC offer (SDP + a short-lived symmetric key). New device scans → a direct peer connection opens → vault streams encrypted device-to-device, no file, no server. **Pro:** the "magic" one-scan transfer. **Con:** WebRTC needs a **signaling** step (usually a tiny relay) and often a STUN/TURN server for NAT traversal — i.e. new infra, and a TURN relay sees (encrypted) traffic. Meaningful build + ops. Cross-network (not same Wi-Fi) is where it gets hard.

chunks.heading: C. Relay-blob via our own service  *(rejected — breaks the promise)*
chunks.text: Old device uploads the encrypted vault to a short-lived slot on our server; QR carries the slot id + decryption key; new device downloads + decrypts. **Rejected:** even though the blob is encrypted, routing the vault through our server erodes the "your data never touches a server" guarantee that is the app's core pitch. Don't.

chunks.heading: Recommendation
chunks.text: Ship **A** first (it's a genuine, safe, testable increment on the existing backup and needs no new infra), and treat **B** as a separate, later project if the one-scan UX is worth the signaling/TURN infrastructure. Do **not** do **C**.

chunks.heading: If we build A — the crypto shape (all headless-testable, mirrors the backup path)
chunks.text: 1. New device generates an ephemeral transfer keypair… **or** simplest: old device generates a random 6–8 word / 32-byte PIN, shows it as a QR. 2. Old device: `encryptBackup(PIN, records)` → file (reuse existing function). 3. New device: scan QR → PIN; user imports file → `decryptBackup(PIN, blob)` (reuse existing). 4. Add a QR generator (small dep or hand-rolled) + a scanner (`BarcodeDetector` where supported, else a camera-frame fallback). Both are UI; the crypto is already tested. **Open decision for Cedric:** A now (safe, small), or commit to B (one-scan, needs signaling + TURN infra)? C is off the table.

chunks.heading: Native wrapper — Capacitor (closed-app dose-reminder sound)
chunks.text: Thin Capacitor shell over the existing static PWA. Reminders run as on-device **LocalNotifications** with a custom sound (`sounds/dose.wav`) so the ding fires when the app is fully closed — on Android **and** iOS. Pure-web/PWA behaviour is unchanged (`cap-notify.js` is a no-op outside the native wrapper). - appId: `com.condaydigital.protocoltracker` · appName: `Protocol Tracker` - webDir: `www/` (regenerated by `sync-www.sh`; gitignored) - `/android`, `/ios`, `www/`, `node_modules/` are **gitignored & regenerated** — the tracked source of truth is: `capacitor.config.json`, `cap-notify.js`, `sounds/dose.wav`, `sync-www.sh`, `apply-native-assets.sh`, `package.json`.

chunks.heading: Fresh clone → project ready (any machine)
chunks.text: ```bash npm install npm run sync # stage www → cap sync → restore native sound asset npx cap add android # (already scaffolded locally; re-run on a fresh clone) bash ./apply-native-assets.sh ```

chunks.heading: Build via GitHub Actions (PRIMARY — no local toolchain needed)
chunks.text: `.github/workflows/android-build.yml` builds a **debug-signed, sideloadable APK** on a runner (the VPS and Chromebook can't build). It regenerates `android/` from scratch (`cap add android` → `apply-native-assets.sh`) → `./gradlew assembleDebug` → uploads `app-debug.apk` as an artifact. Runs on push to `master` (touching wrapper files) or manually via **Actions ▸ Android build ▸ Run workflow**. Download the APK from the run's **Artifacts**, then sideload to the device.

chunks.heading: Prove the closed-app sound (the actual acceptance test)
chunks.text: This needs a human on the physical Android device — a headless box can't hear it. 1. Sideload `app-debug.apk` (enable "install unknown apps" for the file manager). 2. Open the app → **⚙️ Manage ▸ Protocol reminders ▸ 🔔 Enable reminders** (grant the Android 13+ notification permission when prompted). 3. Tap **🔊 Test the reminder sound** → **lock/close the app immediately.** 4. ~15 s later the notification must arrive **and play `dose.wav`** with the app closed. That's the proof. If it's silent, check: notification permission granted? channel `dose` importance not muted by the OS? Do Not Disturb off?

chunks.heading: Android — build & sideload manually (alt: needs Android Studio / SDK; not the VPS)
chunks.text: 1. `npx cap open android` (or open `android/` in Android Studio). 2. Custom sound is at `android/app/src/main/res/raw/dose.wav` (restored by the script). 3. **Exact timing (optional polish):** LocalNotifications uses `allowWhileIdle` (inexact, Doze-safe — usually ±a few min, fine for doses). For to-the-minute firing on Android 12+, add to `AndroidManifest.xml`: `<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM"/>` (note: `USE_EXACT_ALARM` draws Play Store review scrutiny — only if truly needed). 4. **Small icon (optional):** drop a white-on-transparent `ic_stat_icon` into `res/drawable*`; without it Android falls back to the launcher icon (works, less crisp). 5. Build → Build APK (or Run on device). Sideload the APK to the patient's phone. 6. **Test:** open app → enable reminders → Start my day → lock/close app → confirm the ding sounds at a fire time. (For a fast test, set a near-term dose.)

chunks.heading: iOS — build (needs macOS + Xcode + Apple Developer acct for device install)
chunks.text: 1. `npx cap add ios` then `bash ./apply-native-assets.sh` (copies `dose.wav` into `ios/App/App/`). 2. `npx cap open ios` → Xcode ▸ App target ▸ **Build Phases ▸ Copy Bundle Resources** → add `dose.wav` (iOS custom notif sounds must be in the bundle). 3. Signing: select your team; set bundle id `com.condaydigital.protocoltracker`. 4. Run on a physical iPhone (notifications don't fire on Simulator reliably). 5. **Test:** same as Android — enable, start day, close app, hear the ding.

chunks.heading: How it hooks in (for future me)
chunks.text: - `app.js pushSchedule(fires)` now also drives `window.CapNotify.schedule(fires)` when native — same `computeFires()` array feeds both web-push and native. - `enableReminders()` / `renderReminders()` gate on `remindersCapable()` (= `pushSupported() || isNativeWrap()`) so the toggle shows in the wrapper even though web-push `serviceWorker`/`PushManager` are absent there. - `cap-notify.js` creates a high-importance Android channel `dose` with the sound.

chunks.text: > **This is THE current build state. It supersedes every older plan.** If anything you read disagrees with this, THIS wins. > > **Current reality in one line:** the app is a **vanilla-JS, local-first PWA** — **no React Native, no Expo, no app store** — **live at v13**: home → login → onboarding (profile + optional bedtime + supplement picker) → **Start-my-day HERO** (pre-start is a focused ritual; loggers step back) → **T0 schedule engine** (each slot has a tolerance window + live status upcoming→due→overdue→done + relative times; bedtime is a SOFT advisory, never a gate) → water/meals/symptoms → **month-calendar history**. Every surfaced fact is **cited** (📖 Sources card). **Dose reminders** via Web Push (push.condaydigital.com) — enable under ⚙️ Manage. All encrypted at rest. > > **⚠️ ARCHIVED / STALE — ignore these for build-state (they predate the PWA rewrite):**

chunks.text: reminders** via Web Push (push.condaydigital.com) — enable under ⚙️ Manage. All encrypted at rest. > > **⚠️ ARCHIVED / STALE — ignore these for build-state (they predate the PWA rewrite):** > - `PROJECT.md` (Jun 17 — React-Native/Expo era; **moved to `../_stale_predates_pwa_v10/`**). > - `SAVESTATE_medtech.md`, `MEDTECH_GH_CAMPAIGN.md` (Jul 1 — describe the app at commit `2a010d9` with Expo + app-stores + Storybook; **superseded** — banner added at their top). > - Anything mentioning Expo tunnels, app-store launch, Notifee, `.tsx`/Storybook for THIS app = old RN app (preserved only at git tag `v1.1.0`). _Live, working, passable. Full picture below so you can take it over._

chunks.heading: What it is
chunks.text: A **local-first PWA** for the **Coimbra Protocol** (high-dose Vitamin D for MS), built for the patient. No app store, no server for user data — everything is encrypted on the device. Live: - **https://protocoltracker.condaydigital.com** (also cedricconday.github.io/protocol-tracker)

chunks.heading: Where it lives
chunks.text: - **Repo (source of truth):** `CedricConday/protocol-tracker`, VPS clone at `~/workspace/bet/protocol-tracker`. `master` = the PWA. - Old React-Native/Expo app preserved at tag **`v1.1.0`** (`git checkout v1.1.0`) — rich prior art (30+ screens, i18n, native local-notifications, the `schedule_rules`/`daily_anchors` schema). The T0 design + tracker set were ported from it. - Working mirror on chrome-home box: `/home/chrome-home/protocol-tracker/` (not git; edits happen here then rsync to the VPS clone).

chunks.heading: Stack / files (no build step, vanilla JS)
chunks.text: - `index.html` — all screens (home, lock, onboarding profile + supplement picker, dashboard, history). - `app.js` — all logic (one file). Screen router, IndexedDB + crypto, data model, renders, wiring. - `styles.css` — warm cream + Anthropic-coral theme (`--accent #D97757`). - `sw.js` — **network-first** service worker (fresh app on next open) + offline fallback; `push`/`notificationclick` handlers pending (see Notifications). - `manifest.webmanifest`, `icons/` (coral), `build_icons.js` (zero-dep PNG generator). - `content/coimbra/catalog.json` + `timing.json` — supplement catalog (forms/doses) + intake-timing + protocol rules, mined from the patient's CP docs. **Committed** (our derived data). Source PDFs are gitignored under `content/coimbra-source/` (copyright: CP community / Christina Kiening; do NOT publish verbatim). - `test/harness.mjs` — headless e2e (see QA).

chunks.heading: Data model (IndexedDB, all encrypted at rest)
chunks.text: Store `records`, keyed by random `id` (plaintext, not PHI); every other field lives inside AES-GCM ciphertext. Record `type`s: - `profile` (id `'profile'`): name, weight, weightUnit, condition. - `supp`: suppId, name, form, dose, unit, dayParts[], timingKey. - `log` (one per day, id `log-YYYY-MM-DD`): `t0` (Start-my-day timestamp), `planned` (dose count at start, adherence denominator), `taken` map (`{ "suppId::daypart": true }`). - `symptom`: date, ts, label, severity(1–10), note. - `water`: date, ts, ml. - `meal`: date, ts, kind, note. Crypto: **PBKDF2-SHA256 (200k) → AES-GCM-256.** Zero-knowledge (salt + verifier only). Session key held in memory: unlock once, stays until app closed or Lock. **No recovery** (warned at setup) → the encrypted **backup/restore** (Manage) is the safety net.

chunks.heading: Flow
chunks.text: Home → **Log in** (setup passphrase first run) → onboarding **1** profile → **2** supplement picker (pick from catalog → form → dose[required] → which day-parts) → **Dashboard**: **Start my day** sets T0; doses flow forward by `DAYPART_OFFSET` (relative to T0, not wall-clock); check off doses; **Water** (+250/+500, 2.5 L target), **Meals**, **How-you-feel symptoms**. **📅 History** = month calendar; tap a day → full log (doses, water, each meal, each symptom).

chunks.heading: Deploy workflow (keep this exact)
chunks.text: 1. Edit on chrome-home mirror (or directly). 2. `rsync` changed files → VPS clone. 3. `git add <files> && git commit && git push origin master` **from the VPS** (commits are signed there). 4. GitHub Pages rebuilds; the network-first SW + `controllerchange` auto-reload push it to devices on next open. 5. **Bump BOTH** `APP_VERSION` (app.js) and `CACHE` (sw.js) each release — the version shows under **⚙️ Manage** so a stale cache is obvious (currently **v10**). - ⚠️ GitHub Pages **deploy step flakes intermittently** ("Deployment failed, try again later", `error_count: N`) on rapid pushes — the *build* job passes, only *deploy* fails, so it's never our code. `gh run rerun <id> --failed`. If it keeps failing across back-to-back reruns, it's Pages throttling from too many deploys/hour — **wait ~60s, then rerun** (that cleared it on the v13 ship). **Always confirm live**

chunks.heading: Deploy workflow (keep this exact)
chunks.text: --failed`. If it keeps failing across back-to-back reruns, it's Pages throttling from too many deploys/hour — **wait ~60s, then rerun** (that cleared it on the v13 ship). **Always confirm live** afterward (`curl …/app.js | grep APP_VERSION`); a green `run watch` on the build job does NOT mean the deploy succeeded.

chunks.heading: QA — run before every deploy
chunks.text: `cd test && npm i && npm test` → jsdom + real WebCrypto + fake-indexeddb drives the whole flow. **49 checks, suite runs end-to-end** (v13). Add a check when you add a feature. - ⚠️ **Correction (v11, C1):** the pre-v11 "54/54" claim was never real — a stale check expected doses on the dashboard *before* tapping "Start my day", which threw and aborted the suite at ~check 25. Fixed: the harness now taps Start (T0) first. Verify the printed "N/N checks passed" line yourself; don't trust a remembered count.

chunks.heading: Hard-won gotchas (don't re-learn these)
chunks.text: - **Encrypt BEFORE opening the IndexedDB tx.** Awaiting crypto inside a live tx auto-commits it → `TransactionInactiveError` on every write. (`db.put`) - **`db.all` bails if locked** (checks `sessionKey` before AND after the async read) — a render racing the Lock button was decrypting with a null key and crashing. - **Boot guard** (`booted` flag) — a double `DOMContentLoaded` double-wired listeners and double-counted taps. - Day-part chips use **direct per-element listeners** (delegated click was flaky in the test harness). - SW is **network-first for the shell** so updates land; cache-first only for icons.

chunks.heading: What's LEFT (open tasks)
chunks.text: 1. **Notifications (#9) — LIVE (v12, C1, 2026-07-03); only the real-device push delivery remains to be eyeballed.** Web Push driven by T0. - **Service:** systemd unit **`protocol-tracker-push.service`** (enabled, active, `Restart=on-failure`) on `127.0.0.1:8091`; Caddy reverse-proxies **https://push.condaydigital.com** (own TLS). Installer: `push-service/install.sh` (idempotent, re-runnable). DNS `push.condaydigital.com` A → 138.2.162.173 (DNS-only) is live. - **Verified:** public `/health` 200, `/vapid` returns the baked key, CORS allows the Pages origin, and a full `/schedule`→`/health`→`/cancel` round-trip works over HTTPS. - **Client (v12):** SW `push`+`notificationclick` handlers, "🔔 Enable reminders" toggle under ⚙️ Manage, schedule POST on Start-my-day / cancel on Reset. Self-gating (probes `/health` before enabling), zero-PHI, graceful where unsupported. - ⚠️ **NOT yet eyeballed:** an actual push landing on a physical device (needs a real FCM/APNs subscription — do the device test: enable reminders on the phone, Start-my-day, confirm a "Time for your next dose" push arrives).

chunks.heading: What's LEFT (open tasks)
chunks.text: landing on a physical device (needs a real FCM/APNs subscription — do the device test: enable reminders on the phone, Start-my-day, confirm a "Time for your next dose" push arrives). Can't be verified from the VPS. - ⚠️ **Op note:** never leave a manual `node server.js` running — it holds :8091 and makes the systemd unit crash-loop on EADDRINUSE. Use `systemctl` only. (Bit us once on install day.) 2. ~~**Doc citations (#11)**~~ — ✅ **DONE (v11, C1, 2026-07-03).** Every surfaced fact now resolves to a cited source: `content/coimbra/sources.json` (3 docs), `sourceRefs` map in `timing.json`, "Source: …" on timing hints + Protocol-reminders, and a new "📖 Sources & references" card. Disclaimer routed through sources.json. No PDFs republished. 3. **Face ID / passkey unlock (#10)** — instead of Gmail/Apple OAuth (which would break zero-knowledge). WebAuthn/passkey over the existing local key = the

chunks.heading: What's LEFT (open tasks)
chunks.text: routed through sources.json. No PDFs republished. 3. **Face ID / passkey unlock (#10)** — instead of Gmail/Apple OAuth (which would break zero-knowledge). WebAuthn/passkey over the existing local key = the Face-ID convenience, still 100% on-device. 4. Backlog: QR/passkey handshake, richer medical data model (your lane).

chunks.heading: Lane note
chunks.text: Medtech = **your flagship**. C3 built the PWA infra + the tracker set on Cedric's go; the **medical data model / clinical correctness is your call**. Coordinate on `content/coimbra/*` if you refine the protocol data.
